summary: Iteration 3 retest of HackerWare PASSES. Meme Shield fix is now CORRECTLY APPLIED — line 1069 uses `area.classList.add('meme-mode')` so #game-area is no longer renamed. Forced-WIN smoke test across all 5 mini-games (Password, MemeShield, FakeNews, Inbox, Phishing) all produce YOU WIN! feedback with the correct cyber tip. ZERO pageerrors and ZERO console errors during the full playthrough. Instruction text (.pw-title and .phish-instruction) now at top:30px, visually below the HUD time bar (instrTop=332 > hudBottom=302).

issues: Meme Shield tipWin and tipLose are identical strings. Other mini-games may have similar duplication. Minor learning-design polish; not a functional bug.

critical_code_review_comments: Refactor suggestion (non-blocking): helpers localRect() and spawnBurst() hard-code document.getElementById('game-area'). Now that the renaming bug is fixed for both MemeShield and Phishing via classList, consider parameterizing these helpers to accept the area element directly to prevent future regressions if a new mini-game ever needs to set an id.
Defensive ordering (non-blocking): in MemeShield bad-meme click handler, decrement badRemaining BEFORE invoking spawnBurst, so any future FX exception cannot block win logic.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 5 mini-games verified WIN end-to-end with the documented dev hooks: Game.lives=3; Game.miniGameQueue=[MiniGames[N]]; renderLives(); Screens.hud(true); nextRound(); — wait 2.4s for intro. Win sims used: Password→pointerdown/move/up of each .pw-piece to slots[i] (any empty slot accepts within 70px snap); MemeShield→poll-click [data-testid=bad-meme] (resolved at iter 8 in our run); FakeNews→drag fake-news-alert to recycle-bin; Inbox→drag email-virus to trash-zone / email-safe to inbox-zone; Phishing→click [data-testid=phish-line]. Position assertions: HUD bottom=302px, instructionTop=332px (30px gap) for both Meme and Phishing. Dev hooks still all present on window: Game, MiniGames(5), Screens, Timer, nextRound, renderLives, startNewGame, SoundSynth. No outstanding bugs.
rca_of_the_issue: Resolved. Root cause from iteration 2: MemeShield.mount() used `area.id='meme-area'` which broke document.getElementById('game-area') inside spawnBurst()/localRect(). Iteration 3 fix at line 1069 replaces it with `area.classList.add('meme-mode')` (parallel to Phishing fix at line 1382). The pre-existing CSS rule `#game-area.meme-mode { cursor: none; }` (line 350) and cleanup's classList.remove('meme-mode') (line 1186) now match mount(). Additionally `.pw-title` top moved from 14px → 30px (line 329) and `.phish-instruction` top kept at 30px (line 540) for HUD clearance. Verified: 0 pageerrors, 0 console errors, all 5 mini-games winnable.
